questionText: The horizontal displacement of a piston is $s(t) = 24\cos(20t)^\circ - 7\sin(20t)^\circ$ cm, where $t$ is the time in seconds. (a) Express $s(t)$ in the form $R\cos(20t + \alpha)^\circ$, where $R > 0$ and $0^\circ < \alpha < 90^\circ$. (b) State the amplitude and the period of the motion. (c) Find the least displacement and the first time $t > 0$ at which it occurs. (d) Find the first two times $t > 0$ at which the piston passes through $s = 0$, giving your answers in seconds to $3$ decimal places.

steps.explanation: The expression leads with a cosine and subtracts a sine, which is exactly the shape produced by expanding $R\cos(20t+\alpha)$. Choosing this form keeps $\alpha$ acute.
Using $\cos(A+B)=\cos A\cos B - \sin A\sin B$ gives a multiple of $\cos(20t)$ minus a multiple of $\sin(20t)$, matching the original expression.
Matching the $\cos(20t)$ terms gives $R\cos\alpha=24$; matching the $\sin(20t)$ terms, the two minus signs cancel to give $R\sin\alpha=7$.
Squaring and adding removes $\alpha$, giving $R^2 = 576 + 49 = 625$, so the amplitude is $R = 25$ cm.
Dividing the equations gives $\tan\alpha=\tfrac{7}{24}$, and both parts being positive makes $\alpha$ acute, so $\alpha = 16.26^\circ$.
The displacement is now a single cosine wave of amplitude $25$. Its size, timing and zeros are all easy to read off.
The amplitude is the greatest distance the piston moves from the centre of its travel, which is exactly $R = 25$ cm.
The angle grows by $20^\circ$ for each second, so a full cycle of $360^\circ$ takes $18$ seconds. The piston therefore repeats its motion every $18$ s.
A cosine cannot fall below $-1$, so the least displacement is $-25$ cm. No calculus is needed; the minimum is the negative of the amplitude.

steps.description: Find the time of least displacement
steps.explanation: A cosine reaches $-1$ when its angle is $180^\circ$. Solving for $t$ gives the first time the piston is at $-25$ cm.
The piston is at $s=0$ when the cosine is zero. Dividing by $25$ leaves the basic equation $\cos(20t+16.26^\circ)=0$.
Cosine is zero at $90^\circ$ and $270^\circ$ (and every $180^\circ$ after). For the first two positive times we take the two smallest angles above the starting value $16.26^\circ$.
Subtracting the phase and dividing by $20$ gives the first two moments the piston passes through the origin, one on each side of the minimum.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: (a) $s(t) = 25\cos(20t + 16.26^\circ)$. (b) Amplitude $25$ cm and period $18$ s. (c) The least displacement is $-25$ cm, first at $t = 8.187$ s. (d) The piston is at $s = 0$ at $t = 3.687$ s and $t = 12.687$ s.
